20.caption: Load Load1 Critical Threshold
20.description: Exit with CRITICAL status if load average exceed CLOADn; the load average format is the same used by 'uptime' and 'w'

21.caption: Load Load15 Critical Threshold
21.description: Exit with CRITICAL status if load average exceed CLOADn; the load average format is the same used by 'uptime' and 'w'

22.caption: Load Load5 Critical Threshold
22.description: Exit with CRITICAL status if load average exceed CLOADn; the load average format is the same used by 'uptime' and 'w'

23.caption: Load Divide load average by CPU number
23.description: Divide the load averages by the number of CPUs (when possible)

24.caption: Load Load1 Warning Threshold
24.description: Exit with WARNING status if load average exceeds WLOADn

25.caption: Load Load15 Warning Threshold
25.description: Exit with WARNING status if load average exceeds WLOADn

26.caption: Load Load5 Warning Threshold
26.description: Exit with WARNING status if load average exceeds WLOADn

27.caption: Disk Include all paths
27.description: Explicitly select all paths. This is equivalent to -R .*

28.caption: Disk Free space critical threshold
28.description: Exit with CRITICAL status if less than INTEGER units of disk are free or Exit with CRITCAL status if less than PERCENT of disk space is free

30.caption: Disk Include path or partitions (regex case insensitive)
30.description: Case insensitive regular expression for path/partition (may be repeated)

31.caption: Disk Display only devices with errors

32.caption: Disk Check for exact paths
32.description: For paths or partitions specified with -p, only check for exact paths

33.caption: Disk Ignored filesystem types
33.description: Ignore all filesystems of indicated type (may be repeated)

34.caption: Disk Ignore path or partitions (regex case sensitive)
34.description: Regular expression to ignore selected path or partition (may be repeated)

35.caption: Disk Ignore path or partitions (regex case insensitive)
35.description: Regular expression to ignore selected path/partition (case insensitive) (may be repeated)

36.caption: Disk Exclude root-reserved blocks
36.description: Don't account root-reserved blocks into freespace in perfdata

37.caption: Disk Free inode critical threshold (%)
37.description: Exit with CRITICAL status if less than PERCENT of inode space is free

38.caption: Disk Free inode warning threshold (%)
38.description: Exit with WARNING status if less than PERCENT of inode space is free

42.caption: Disk Check remote file systems for accessibility
42.description: Only check local filesystems against thresholds. Yet call stat on remote filesystems to test if they are accessible (e.g. to detect Stale NFS Handles)

44.caption: Disk Space unit
44.description: Choose bytes, kB, MB, GB, TB (default: MB)

45.caption: Disk Free space warning threshold
45.description: Exit with WARNING status if less than INTEGER units of disk are free or Exit with WARNING status if less than PERCENT of disk space is free

125.caption: Process Argument
125.description: Only scan for processes with args that contain STRING

52.caption: Process Critical
52.description: Generate critical state if metric is outside this range

53.caption: Process Warning
53.description: Generate warning state if metric is outside this range

46.caption: Memory Consider OS cache as free memory
46.description: Count OS caches as FREE memory

51.caption: Process Command
51.description: Only scan for exact matches of COMMAND (without path)

54.caption: Swap Compare partitions one by one
54.description: Conduct comparisons for all swap partitions, one by one

55.caption: Swap Free Critical Threshold
55.description: he free swap space critical threshold in % (enable swap_integer for number values). Defaults to 25%.

56.caption: Swap Thresholds are bytes (not percentages)
56.description: Specifies whether the thresholds are passed as number or percent value. Defaults to false (percent values).

57.caption: Swap Free Warning Threshold
57.description: The free swap space warning threshold in % (enable swap_integer for number values). Defaults to 50%.

58.caption: SystemD Restart Unit on issues
58.description: Enable unit restart in case of issues (see "SystemD Restart Unit if failed")

59.caption: SystemD Restart Unit if failed
59.description:
If disabled, restart the unit if it is not active.
If enabled, restart the unit if it is in a failed state.
(see "SystemD Restart Unit on issues")

60.caption: SystemD Do not check autostartup
60.description: Do not return CRITICAL if the unit is not enabled

61.caption: SystemD Unit Name
61.description: Name of the SystemD Unit to check (with or without .service suffix)

66.caption: Load Critical threshold
66.description:
Represents a Critical Threshold.
Possible formats are "NUMBER" or "NUMBER%", "[LOW-HIGH]" or "[LOW%-HIGH%]", where NUMBER, LOW and HIGH are decimals numbers (E.g.: 67, 67%, [50-80], [50%80%]).
Critical is returned if VALUE>NUMBER or LOW<NUMBER<HIGH.
It can be prefixed with "!" (exclamation dot), which operates as a negation inverting the logic (E.g.: !67, !67%, ![50-80], ![50%80%]).

67.caption: Load Warning threshold
67.description:
Represents a Warning Threshold.
Possible formats are "NUMBER" or "NUMBER%", "[LOW-HIGH]" or "[LOW%-HIGH%]", where NUMBER, LOW and HIGH are decimals numbers (E.g.: 67, 67%, [50-80], [50%80%]).
Warning is returned if VALUE>NUMBER or LOW<NUMBER<HIGH.
It can be prefixed with "!" (exclamation dot), which operates as a negation inverting the logic (E.g.: !67, !67%, ![50-80], ![50%80%]).

71.caption: Disk Show used space
71.description: Work with used instead of free space

72.caption: Disk unit
72.description: Use this unit to display disk space

74.caption: Memory Critical threshold
74.description:
Represents a Critical Threshold.
Possible formats are "NUMBER" , "[LOW-HIGH]", where NUMBER, LOW and HIGH are decimals numbers (E.g.: 67, 67%, [50-80], [50%80%]).
Critical is returned if VALUE>NUMBER or LOW<NUMBER<HIGH.
It can be prefixed with "!" (exclamation dot), which operates as a negation inverting the logic (E.g.: !67, !67%, ![50-80], ![50%80%]).

75.caption: Memory Show used space
75.description: Show used memory instead of the free memory

76.caption: Memory Unit
76.description: Use this unit to display value and interpret thresholds

77.caption: Memory Warning threshold
77.description:
Represents a Warning Threshold.
Possible formats are "NUMBER" , "[LOW-HIGH]", where NUMBER, LOW and HIGH are decimals numbers (E.g.: 67, 67%, [50-80], [50%80%]).
Warning is returned if VALUE>NUMBER or LOW<NUMBER<HIGH.
It can be prefixed with "!" (exclamation dot), which operates as a negation inverting the logic (E.g.: !67, !67%, ![50-80], ![50%80%]).

80.caption: Performance counter Output label
80.description: Use this instead of the counter name in the perfomance data

82.caption: Performance counter sampling interval
82.description: Wait time between two counter collections in ms

85.caption: Processes owner name
85.description: Count only procs of this user

87.caption: [NX] PowerShell ExecutionPolicies List
87.description: Set the default execution policy for the session.

88.caption: Powershell Process Name
88.description: Name of the process to check

89.caption: CScript Enable Batch mode
89.description: Batch mode: Suppresses script errors and prompts from displaying

92.caption: Script file path
92.description: Text file containing VBS commands to be execute. You must include the extension (normally .vbs)

95.caption: Powershell Start non-interactive
95.description: Don't present an interactive prompt to the user.

96.caption: Powershell Do not load User profile
96.description: Do not load the PowerShell profile. No pre-defined functions will be available. When setting up a scheduled job, using -NoProfile can be a quick way to document the fact that nothing special in the profile is needed. It also ensures that any future profile changes will not affect the job.

98.caption: Script file path
98.description: Execute a script file. The filename is required. If the file/pathname contains spaces then surround with double quotation marks:  -file "path to\your script.ps1"

100.caption: Warn instead of Critical when stopped
100.description: Warn instead of critical when service is not running

102.caption: Swap Show used swap
102.description: Show used swap instead of the free swap

105.caption: Ignore if an update requires reboot
105.description: Do not automatically return critical if an update requiring reboot is present.

106.caption: Update critical threshold
106.description: Number of updates to trigger a critical

107.caption: Consider "May need a reboot" as "Need a reboot"
107.description: Treat "update may need reboot" as "update needs reboot"

108.caption: Update warning threshold
108.description: Number of updates to trigger a warning

118.caption: Business Process Consider ACK as OK
118.description: Treat acknowledged hosts/services always as UP/OK

119.caption: Business Process Name
119.description: Name of the Business Process containing the Node to check

120.caption: Business Process Node to check
120.description: Name of the Node to check

121.caption: Business Process Consider Downtime as OK
121.description: Treat hosts/services in downtime always as UP/OK

122.caption: Business Process Show colored states
122.description: Paints state's name with state's color

123.caption: Business Process Show details
123.description: Show problem details as a tree

124.caption: Business Process State type to consider
124.description: Define which state type to look at. Could be either soft or hard, overrides an eventually configured default

19.caption: SNMP Version
19.description: Version of SNMP used by the device

112.caption: SNMP Critical threshold
112.description:
Critical threshold range(s) in NAGIOS Format.
When checking multiple OIDs, separate ranges by commas like '1:10,1:,:20'.

113.caption: SNMP Prefix label
113.description: Prefix label for output from plugin

115.caption: SNMP Warning threshold
115.description:
Warning threshold range(s) in NAGIOS Format.
When checking multiple OIDs, separate ranges by commas like '-w 1:10,1:,:20'.

126.caption: [NX] Behavior on Icinga agent Disconnected
126.description: Describes what happens in case Icinga2 Agent becomes unresponsive.

130.caption: SNMP oid
130.description: Object identifier(s) or SNMP variables whose value you wish to query

131.caption: SNMP String
131.description: Return OK state (for that OID) if STRING is an exact match

132.caption: [NX] ITIL Urgency
132.description: ITIL urgency used on Business Process

133.caption: [NX] ITIL Impact
133.description: ITIL impact used on Business Process

144.caption: Icinga Instance Minimum Allowed Version
144.description: Required minimum Icinga 2 version, e.g. 2.8.0. If not satisfied, the state changes to Critical. Release packages only.

161.caption: [NX] Recipient groups list Override
161.description: Overrides Recepient groups list from owning Host Object

162.caption: [NX] Recipients list Override
162.description: Override Recepients from onwing Host Object

163.caption: [NX] Notification's mode
163.description: Determines the way notifications are sent for the Service

164.caption: [NX] Severity
164.description: Severity level for host or service

165.caption: [NX] Service behavior on Host Down List
165.description: Describes what happens in case one the Host containing this Service goes down.

settings.datalist: [NX] Service behavior on Host Down List

166.caption: [NX] Active channel override
166.description: Uses the provided Notification Channels instead of the ones selected in the Host Object

167.caption: [NX] NetEye Tenant
167.description: Tenant name where search data (like namespace for ElasticAgent)

583.caption: [NX] Netstat Critical
583.description: Comma separated. Critical level(s) (if more than one attrib, must have multiple values). Critical values can have the same prefix modifiers as warning (see above) except '^'

584.caption: [NX] Netstat Port
584.description: Comma separated. Which tcp ports (attributes) to check. The value here can be either numeric or name that would be come from /etc/services. The value should be prefixed with: > : check outgoing TCP connections, < : check incoming TCP connections (default). Special value of '>@' or just '>' allow to specify that you want to check on total number of TCP connections (this is reported in perfomance data as 'all_out').

585.caption: [NX] Netstat Warning
585.description: Comma separated. Warning level(s) - usually numbers (same number of values specified as number of attributes). Warning values can have the following prefix modifiers: > : warn if data is above this value (default), < : warn if data is below this value, = : warn if data is equal to this value, ! : warn if data is not equal to this value, ~ : do not check this data (must not be followed by number), ^ : this disables checks that warning is less than critical

[NX] Basic notifications Settings (Advan.description: Advanced settings for Basic NEP Notifications

[NX] Basic notifications Settings.description: Common settings for Basic NEP Notifications
